title: Static Web App
description: CDN-delivered frontend architecture. Route 53 routes traffic to CloudFront, which caches and serves assets from a private S3 bucket. ACM provides SSL/TLS. This pattern serves SPAs, static sites, and media assets globally with sub-10ms latency at the edge — no servers required.
practices: Keep S3 private — use CloudFront Origin Access Control (OAC) to grant CloudFront-only read access. Never set the bucket ACL to public or enable static website hosting on the bucket itself.
ACM certificates for CloudFront must be provisioned in us-east-1, regardless of where your application region is.
Set viewer_protocol_policy to redirect-to-https — never serve content over plain HTTP in production.
Use long TTLs for static assets (images, JS, CSS) and short TTLs for HTML entry points. Content-hash your asset filenames so cache invalidation is cheap.
Route 53 ALIAS records are required for apex domains (example.com). CNAME only works for subdomains (www.example.com).
CloudFront distributions take 10-20 minutes to deploy globally — factor this into your CI/CD pipeline.
configNotes: CloudFront origin_id must reference the S3 node ID.
CloudFront acm_certificate_id must reference the ACM node ID.
S3 block_public_access should be true — access is through CloudFront only.
Route 53 hosted_zone_name should match your ACM domain_name.
